resume:
RACHEL VOSS
VP of Sales — Enterprise SaaS
rachel.voss.sales@example.com

PROFILE
Enterprise sales leader with 13 years scaling go-to-market motions in B2B SaaS. I have built and led teams through hypergrowth and turnaround, owning new-logo and expansion revenue.

EXPERIENCE

VP of Sales — Cobalt Analytics (2021–present)
Lead the global enterprise sales org for a data-analytics platform.
- Team size: I lead a team of 35 quota-carrying reps (summary) — though a later section states "built and scaled the team from 12 to 60."
- Grew annual recurring revenue from $40M to $95M (this section) / cited elsewhere as "took the business from roughly $50M to over $100M."
- Tenure note: "Joined Cobalt in early 2021" here, but the GTM-Transformation project below says it ran "over 2019–2021 under my leadership at Cobalt."
- Implemented MEDDICC across the org and rebuilt the sales-comp plan.
- Closed the company's largest-ever deal, a multi-year contract with a global bank.

GTM Transformation (project highlight)
- Led a go-to-market transformation that shifted the org from a geography model to an industry-vertical model, said to run over 2019–2021 under my leadership at Cobalt — predating the stated 2021 start date.

Senior Director, Sales — Vantage Cloud (2016–2021)
- Owned the Americas commercial segment.
- Improved net revenue retention from 105% to 121%.
- Reduced average sales cycle from 120 to 78 days.

Regional Sales Manager — Helios Software (2012–2016)
- Managed a team of 8 reps in the Midwest.
- President's Club two years running.

SKILLS
Enterprise GTM strategy, sales-team building, MEDDICC/forecasting, comp-plan design, vertical go-to-market, executive negotiation, NRR/expansion motion, pipeline management.

EDUCATION
B.A. Economics.
jd: Helix Data is hiring a VP of Sales to lead our enterprise go-to-market as we scale past $60M ARR. Reporting to the CRO, you'll own new-logo and expansion revenue, build and develop a quota-carrying team, and drive a forecasting discipline the board can trust. We are looking for a leader who has personally driven a specific, measurable revenue transformation — and who can narrate it cleanly: the starting state, the diagnosis, the specific motions you installed, and the quantified outcome with a timeline. We will press on consistency; if the numbers or dates on your resume move, we will ask you to reconcile them on the spot. MEDDICC fluency, comp-plan design experience, and a track record of executive-level deal-making are expected.

history.q: Rachel, give me the shape of your current role at Cobalt — team size, what revenue you own.
Your resume says 35 reps in one place and 'scaled from 12 to 60' in another. Which is the real number today?
Same on ARR — I see $40M to $95M and also '$50M to over $100M.' Reconcile those for me.
There's also a date issue: you list a 2021 start at Cobalt, but the GTM transformation is dated 2019–2021 'under your leadership at Cobalt.' When did you actually join?
Let's go deep on the GTM transformation — the shift from geography to vertical model. What did you personally drive?
How did you sequence that change so you didn't tank in-flight pipeline during the reorg?
Take that transformation end to end: the starting state, the specific motions you installed, and the measured result with a timeline.

candidate_last_answer: Sure. So what I did was install a vertical coverage model — I carved the team into industry pods, hired in two seasoned reps from banking and healthcare, layered MEDDICC on top so the forecast got real, and rebuilt comp to reward multi-year and expansion. The pods ramped, the pipeline got higher quality, and within about three quarters we were closing bigger, stickier deals — we ended up roughly doubling the business. It was the right call and the board loved it.

top_question_traits: expected-yield: the missing Situation baseline and a committed outcome — the exact starting ARR and rep count before the vertical reorg, the precise ending figure, and the window — so 'roughly doubling' resolves to hard numbers consistent with one of the conflicting resume claims
